title: dianthus ciliatus subsp. dalmaticus
intro: Dianthus ciliatus subsp. dalmaticus, also known as Dalmatian pink or Dalmatian carnation, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Dalmatian pink has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and providing antimicrobial protection. Dalmatian pink can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other minor infections.
